steps.title: Find the slope of the tangent line
steps.content: A tangent line is perpendicular to the radius at the point of tangency. The radius from center $(-1,1)$ to $(7,-6)$ has slope $\dfrac{-6-1}{7-(-1)}=\dfrac{-7}{8}$, so the tangent slope is the negative reciprocal: $\dfrac{8}{7}$.

steps.title: Write the equation of line t
steps.content: Using point-slope form through $(7,-6)$: $y-(-6)=\dfrac{8}{7}(x-7)$, which simplifies to $y=\dfrac{8}{7}x-8-6=\dfrac{8}{7}x-14$.

steps.title: Test each point in the line equation
